## All joking aside, we need that sick shader 

The plan is to bring in a shader based off one by Paper Design into the project. 

We will have to install this npm package `@paper-design/shaders-react`

### Goal

Our goal here is to bring in the shader as an async import **after** the DOM has loaded. 
That way it won't effect our Lighthouse scores, we need those to be great.

The shader will appear **below** all of the content, but **above** the background svg image.
We will try only loading it on the landing page route "/" first. 
Then we will try other routes as well to see how they look.

### Base TSX Shader Code

Below is the code for the liquid metal shader from Paper.
With the `image` prop, maybe we can change that to our background image based on screen width?
The effect may be better with it like that, otherwise remove the `image` prop.

With the `style` prop, we can see how the color looks or try `transparent`.
We will have to adjust the height and width props to make it fit the screen. 
We can use responsive units: vh, vw, %.

```tsx
/** @paper-design/shaders-react@0.0.60 */
import { Water as Water1 } from "@paper-design/shaders-react";

/**
 * Code exported from Paper
 * https://app.paper.design/file/01K92ZHE2X2BHKNQZNDTY30VNA?node=01K930F3B8W8WY8HAQAW8NEXT2
 * on Nov 2, 2025 at 2:27 PM.
 */
export default function Water() {
  return (
    <Water1
      speed={0.54}
      colorBack="#00000000"
      colorHighlight="#ffffff"
      size={0.74}
      highlights={0.07}
      layering={0.5}
      edges={0.8}
      waves={0.25}
      caustic={0.1}
      image="https://workers.paper.design/file-assets/01K92ZHE2X2BHKNQZNDTY30VNA/01K930H8R6SSEHVYM8N4E02SPT.svg"
      scale={1.43}
      fit="contain"
      style={{ backgroundColor: "#909090", height: "320px", width: "480px" }}
    />
  );
}
```

#### Checklist
- [ ] - App builds
- [ ] - Tests pass
- [ ] - Approve visual effects myself.

## Comprehensive TanStack Start Landing Page & Layout Structure Report

### File Paths & Architecture

**Route Definition:**
- `/home/peterc/ccw/apps/frontend/src/routes/index.tsx` - Landing page route (loads HomePage component)
- `/home/peterc/ccw/apps/frontend/src/routes/__root.tsx` - Root layout (TanStack router root with context)

**Page Component:**
- `/home/peterc/ccw/apps/frontend/src/pages/Home/Home.tsx` - HomePage component (main landing page)
- `/home/peterc/ccw/apps/frontend/src/pages/Home/Home.css.ts` - Landing page styles (vanilla-extract)

**Global Layout & Shell:**
- `/home/peterc/ccw/apps/frontend/src/components/GlobalLayout/GlobalLayout.tsx` - Root shell component (HTML structure)
- `/home/peterc/ccw/apps/frontend/src/components/GlobalLayout/GlobalLayout.css.ts` - Global background styling

**Supporting CSS & Theme:**
- `/home/peterc/ccw/apps/frontend/src/styles/globals.css.ts` - Global CSS loader (ensures vanilla-extract isn't tree-shaken)
- `/home/peterc/ccw/apps/frontend/src/styles/theme.css.ts` - Design tokens and theme contract
- `/home/peterc/ccw/apps/frontend/src/styles.css` - Tailwind + DM Sans base styles

**Background SVG Assets:**
- `/home/peterc/ccw/apps/frontend/public/beach_backdrop.svg` - Desktop light theme background
- `/home/peterc/ccw/apps/frontend/public/mobile_backdrop.svg` - Mobile light theme background
- `/home/peterc/ccw/apps/frontend/public/night-backdrop.svg` - Desktop dark theme background
- `/home/peterc/ccw/apps/frontend/public/mobile_night.svg` - Mobile dark theme background

---

### Component Hierarchy (Root to Landing Page)

```
html (GlobalLayout)
  ├─ head
  │   ├─ HeadContent (router-managed)
  │   └─ FavIcons
  └─ body
      ├─ <div className={globalBackground} /> (BACKGROUND LAYER)
      ├─ Skip-to-content link (accessibility)
      ├─ ErrorBoundary
      │   ├─ Header
      │   ├─ <main id="app-root">
      │   │   └─ <Outlet />  ← Routes render here
      │   │       └─ HomePage (landing page route)
      │   │           ├─ HeroSection
      │   │           ├─ divider
      │   │           ├─ ContentCardsSection
      │   │           ├─ divider
      │   │           └─ SocialLinksRow
      │   ├─ Footer
      │   ├─ TanStackDevtools
      │   └─ ClientOnly (Preview UI, ExitPreviewButton)
      └─ <Scripts />
```

---

### Background SVG Layering & Z-Index System

#### Fixed Background Positioning (GlobalLayout.css.ts)

The `globalBackground` div uses **fixed positioning** with **z-index: -1** to sit behind all content:

```css
.globalBackground {
  position: fixed;
  top: 0;
  left: 0;
  width: 100vw;
  height: 100vh;
  z-index: -1;  /* CRITICAL: Behind all content */
  margin: 0;
  padding: 0;
}
```

#### Light/Dark Theme Switching

Two SVG backgrounds are layered using **pseudo-elements** (::before and ::after):

1. **Base Layer (::element itself):** Light theme SVG
   - Mobile: `url('/mobile_backdrop.svg')`
   - Desktop (768px+): `url('/mobile_backdrop.svg')` at `left 60%`
   - Large Desktop (1024px+): `url('/beach_backdrop.svg')` at `top left`

2. **Dark Overlay Layer (::before):** Dark theme SVG
   - Mobile: `url('/mobile_night.svg')`
   - Desktop: `url('/mobile_night.svg')` at `left 60%`
   - Large Desktop: `url('/night-backdrop.svg')`
   - **Default opacity: 0** (hidden)
   - **Opacity: 1 when `[data-theme="dark"]`** (visible in dark mode)
   - Smooth transition: `opacity 0.6s ease`

3. **Optional Gradient Overlay (::after):** Empty pseudo-element
   - `pointerEvents: none` (doesn't block interactions)
   - Currently has no background (see commented-out gradient)
   - Can add overlays without blocking clicks

#### Responsive Behavior

```
Mobile (< 768px):
  Light: mobile_backdrop.svg at "top left"
  Dark:  mobile_night.svg at "top left"

Tablet (768px - 1023px):
  Light: mobile_backdrop.svg at "left 60%"
  Dark:  mobile_night.svg at "left 60%"

Desktop (1024px+):
  Light: beach_backdrop.svg at "top left"
  Dark:  night-backdrop.svg at "top left"
```

---

### Content Positioning Relative to Background

**Body Background:**
- `background: transparent` in GlobalLayout.css.ts (so the fixed background shows through)
- `display: flex; flex-direction: column; min-height: 100vh` in styles.css
- Fallback: Light gray (`#f5f7fa` in light mode, `#0f1214` in dark mode) from styles.css body rule

# Plan: Add Water Shader to Landing Page (Issue #27)

## Context

The site has a fixed background SVG (`z-index: -1`) and content on top (normal stacking). The goal is to add a Paper Design Water shader that sits **between** the background SVG and the page content — visible but not blocking interaction. It must lazy-load after DOM paint to preserve Lighthouse scores.

## Steps

### 1. Install the shader package
```bash
npm install @paper-design/shaders-react --workspace=@santan/frontend
```

### 2. Create the WaterShader component

**New file:** `apps/frontend/src/components/WaterShader/WaterShader.tsx`

- Default-export a component that renders the `Water` shader from `@paper-design/shaders-react`
- Props based on the issue's sample code (speed, colorBack, size, etc.)
- Remove the `image` prop initially (issue says to try without it)
- Style: `width: 100vw`, `height: 100vh`, `backgroundColor: transparent` or a subtle color
- This is the chunk boundary — the dynamic import targets this file

**New file:** `apps/frontend/src/components/WaterShader/WaterShader.css.ts`

- `position: fixed`, `top: 0`, `left: 0`, `width: 100vw`, `height: 100vh`
- `z-index: 0` — above the background SVG (`z-index: -1`) but below content (normal flow)
- `pointer-events: none` — so it doesn't block clicks on content above it
- `opacity` transition for a smooth fade-in when it loads

### 3. Lazy-load in the Home page

**Modify:** `apps/frontend/src/pages/Home/Home.tsx`

- Add `React.lazy(() => import("@/components/WaterShader/WaterShader.tsx"))`
- Use a `useEffect` + `useState` to defer rendering until after initial paint (so the shader bundle doesn't block first contentful paint)
- Wrap in `<Suspense fallback={null}>` — same pattern as `PreviewWrapper`
- Render the shader **before** the `homeContainer` div so it layers underneath content

### 4. Adjust content z-index if needed

The `homeContainer` has `background: transparent` which is good — the shader will show through. If content cards aren't readable over the shader, we may need to bump their `position: relative` + `z-index: 1` — but this depends on visual review.

## Files to modify
- `apps/frontend/package.json` — new dependency (via npm install)
- `apps/frontend/src/pages/Home/Home.tsx` — lazy import + render shader
- **New:** `apps/frontend/src/components/WaterShader/WaterShader.tsx`
- **New:** `apps/frontend/src/components/WaterShader/WaterShader.css.ts`

## Verification
1. `npm run build` — app builds successfully
2. `npm test --workspace=@santan/frontend` — tests pass
3. `npm run dev` — start dev server, visit `localhost:3000`
4. Visually confirm: shader renders below content, above background SVG
5. Confirm shader does NOT appear on other routes (e.g. `/about`)
6. Check Lighthouse — shader bundle should not appear in critical path

```
z-index:  0  │  Water shader (fixed, pointer-events: none)    ← NEW
z-index:  1+ │  Page content (normal flow)                    ← existing
```

### 1. Install the package

```bash

```

### 2. Create `apps/frontend/src/components/WaterShader/WaterShader.css.ts`

Vanilla Extract style for the shader container:
- `position: fixed`, `top: 0`, `left: 0`, `width: 100vw`, `height: 100vh`
- `zIndex: 0` — above the SVG backdrop (`-1`) but below normal-flow content
- `pointerEvents: 'none'` — non-interactive overlay
- Opacity transition: start at `0`, animate to `1` for a smooth fade-in after load

### 3. Create `apps/frontend/src/components/WaterShader/WaterShader.tsx`

Default-export a component that:
- Renders `<Water>` from `@paper-design/shaders-react`
- Passes `style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%' }}` to fill its container
- Uses the shader container style from step 2
- Uses a `useState` + `useEffect` to flip opacity from 0 to 1 after mount (fade-in)
- Props from the issue sample: `speed`, `colorBack`, `colorHighlight`, `highlights`, `layering`, `edges`, `waves`, `caustic`, `size` — no `image` prop initially
- Marks the container `aria-hidden="true"`

### 4. Modify `apps/frontend/src/pages/Home/Home.tsx`

In the `HomePage` component (not the inner `Home` — it needs to render regardless of preview mode):

- Add `React.lazy(() => import("@/components/WaterShader/WaterShader.tsx"))` — follows the existing `PreviewWrapper` lazy pattern on line 17

```tsx
)}
```

content.text:
Tests failed because Playwright browsers aren't installed — that's a pre-existing environment issue, not related to our changes.

Here's a summary of what's done:

**Implementation complete.** Here's what was changed:

1. **Installed** `@paper-design/shaders-react` in the frontend workspace
2. **Created** `apps/frontend/src/components/WaterShader/WaterShader.css.ts` — fixed-position container at `z-index: 0` with `pointer-events: none` and opacity fade-in transition
3. **Created** `apps/frontend/src/components/WaterShader/WaterShader.tsx` — renders the Water shader full-viewport, fades in after mount via `requestAnimationFrame`
4. **Modified** `apps/frontend/src/pages/Home/Home.tsx` — lazy-loads the shader after first paint using `useEffect` + `useState` gate, wrapped in `<Suspense fallback={null}>`

**Verification results:**
- Build passes — shader is code-split into its own 25.72 kB chunk (`WaterShader-BzdLsdCX.js`)
- No type errors in our files (the `vite.config.ts` error is pre-existing)
- Playwright tests fail due to missing browser binaries (pre-existing env issue)

To visually test, start the dev server with `npm run dev` and check `localhost:3000`. You'll want to verify the shader looks good and that content remains clickable on top of it.

OK — WebGL is correctly detected as unsupported (`hasWebGL: false`), so our guard works. The problem is timing: React.lazy loads the module first, and the shader library's module-level code or constructor runs before our component's `useEffect` check fires. The `Water` component is being instantiated during render, before the effect.

I need to move the WebGL check to happen **before** we even lazy-load the component:
I'll move the WebGL check into the `HomePage` component's `useEffect`, so the shader module is never loaded if WebGL isn't available:

content.text:
Zero errors. The fix was moving the WebGL check **upstream** into `HomePage`'s `useEffect` — now if WebGL isn't available, we never set `showShader = true`, so the shader module is never lazy-loaded at all. No bundle download, no instantiation, no errors.

To summarize what we've done for issue #27:

1. **Installed** `@paper-design/shaders-react`
2. **Created** `WaterShader` component with fade-in animation
3. **Lazy-loaded** it in `HomePage` with deferred rendering after first paint
4. **Fixed z-index layering** — shader at `z-index: 0`, content at `z-index: 1`, header already at `z-index: 100`
5. **Added WebGL guard** — browsers without WebGL skip the shader entirely (no errors, no wasted bundle download)

Want to check how it looks in your regular Chrome, or should we commit?

- Picks the right SVG based on screen width (1024px breakpoint) and theme (light/dark)
- Reacts to theme toggles via a `MutationObserver` on `data-theme`
- Updates on window resize to swap mobile/desktop SVGs
